title: organization — field classification
description: Organization rows are structural metadata about a customer's business — brand names, colors, categories, contact points. Almost no PII. The one PII-adjacent column is `contact_person_name` (a designated org-level contact, typically an employee of the tenant). Every other column is either public (visible to any tenant member) or internal (ops-only). No fields ever cross into `regulated_minor` / `regulated_health` / `regulated_financial` because Organization is above the domain plane where such data lives.
notes: `contact_person_name` is the one PII-adjacent field. It is a role designation (e.g. 'Head of Operations') that names an individual for external correspondence. Under GDPR it is personal data — classified `internal` (not public) so it appears only to authorised tenant members + platform admins. Never exposed to unauthenticated surfaces.

`support_email` + `support_phone` are legally the tenant's business contact — visible to the tenant's own members + to platform admins under contract. Classified `internal` for consistency with contact_person_name.

`tree_path` is `internal` because it embeds a chain of internal ULIDs — revealing it publicly would leak the tenant's internal structural size + shape. The wire projection returns `tree_depth` (safe) but NOT `tree_path` (unsafe unless the caller is authenticated to the tenant).
The `audit_log` list is fields whose changes MUST be captured by `owen-it/laravel-auditing`. Reparent + default-flip + status transition + branding change are compliance-material.
`sort_order` is public because it directly drives the UI picker order — no confidentiality concern; a competitor knowing that tenant X sorts Elite Sports above Community Sports is a nothingburger.
